content.claim: Hungary maintains a dense secondary school network (880 schools, 2024/25) with the goal that every student can access a school by public transport in under one hour, which mitigates transport and access-cost barriers to participating in the tracking system.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Dense physical infrastructure does not address demand-side barriers (tutoring costs, cultural capital, application fees) that families must overcome to compete for entry
Network density is a structural fact; utilization and equity of access across socioeconomic groups is not specified
The claim addresses access to *schools*, not access to selective tracks within schools
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
